parents.father_research_notes: Deacon Richard Hale (1717-1802) of Coventry CT — paternal grandfather Samuel Hale (1687-1723) of Beverly MA. The Coventry Hales are a separate family from the Wales-Hartford Hales (Samuel Hale Sr 1637) that married into the Talcott line via Sarah Talcott + Jonathan Hale 1717.
parents.mother_research_notes: Elizabeth Strong Hale of Northampton MA, daughter of Joseph Strong Jr (1701-1773) and Elizabeth Strong (1704-1792). Strong family of Northampton.

career.organization: First Congregational Church of West Hampton
career.notes: First minister of West Hampton Massachusetts.

narrative_blocks.header: Brother of Nathan Hale the patriot martyr
narrative_blocks.body: Elder brother of Capt Nathan Hale (1755-1776) — the Continental Army officer hanged by the British at New York September 22, 1776 with the final words 'I only regret that I have but one life to lose for my country.' Enoch survived him by 61 years and shaped his own son's name around the family memory.

narrative_blocks.header: First minister of West Hampton Massachusetts
narrative_blocks.body: Settled at West Hampton MA as the new parish's first minister, where he served the gathered congregation through his long life. Daughter Octavia (1788-1867) born at Westhampton would marry William Hooker Jr there December 19, 1811 and carry the Hale-Hooker connection into the Westfield and Hartford banking families.

parents.father_research_notes: Deacon Richard Hale (1717-1802) of Coventry CT — paternal grandfather Samuel Hale (1687-1723) of Beverly MA. The Coventry Hales are a separate family from the Wales-Hartford Hales (Samuel Hale Sr 1637) that married into the Talcott line via Sarah Talcott + Jonathan Hale 1717.
parents.mother_research_notes: Elizabeth Strong Hale of Northampton MA, daughter of Joseph Strong Jr (1701-1773) and Elizabeth Strong (1704-1792). Strong family of Northampton.

career.organization: First Congregational Church of West Hampton
career.notes: First minister of West Hampton Massachusetts.

narrative_blocks.header: Brother of Nathan Hale the patriot martyr
narrative_blocks.body: Elder brother of Capt Nathan Hale (1755-1776) — the Continental Army officer hanged by the British at New York September 22, 1776 with the final words 'I only regret that I have but one life to lose for my country.' Enoch survived him by 61 years and shaped his own son's name around the family memory.

narrative_blocks.header: First minister of West Hampton Massachusetts
narrative_blocks.body: Settled at West Hampton MA as the new parish's first minister, where he served the gathered congregation through his long life. Daughter Octavia (1788-1867) born at Westhampton would marry William Hooker Jr there December 19, 1811 and carry the Hale-Hooker connection into the Westfield and Hartford banking families.

parents.mother_research_notes: Octavia Throop Hale (1754-1839) of Bozrah CT, died Westhampton MA aged 85. Not yet built (deferred).

cross_connections.display_label: Uncle executed as Continental Army spy, September 1776

narrative_blocks.header: Niece of Nathan Hale the patriot martyr
narrative_blocks.body: Her father Rev Enoch Hale of Westhampton MA was elder brother of Capt Nathan Hale — the Continental Army officer hanged as spy by the British at New York September 22, 1776. Nathan died nearly twelve years before her birth; the family memory of the executed uncle shaped her own brother's name (Nathan Hale Sr 1784-1863) and reached into her children's generation through her son's cousin marriage to her sister Sybella Hale Hall's daughter Sarah.

narrative_blocks.header: Died of dementia paralytica at Northampton, 1867
narrative_blocks.body: Died December 21, 1867 at Northampton MA age 79 — eighteen months after her husband — with cause given on her death record as 'Dementile Paralysis,' the period name for dementia paralytica, the late-stage neurological syndrome of progressive cognitive and motor decline.

parents.father_research_notes: Dr. William Hooker (H00529, 1766-?), physician + trader at West Hampton MA; born Northampton MA Nov 26, 1766; son of Rev John and Sarah Worthington Hooker of Northampton. Per EH 1909 #529: 'William Hooker (John* John* John,3 Samuel,2 Thomas1)...m. 1798 Anna Thompson, daughter of Hugh Thompson of East Windsor CT. She died at West Hampton MA. He married No. 2, Widow of Ensign Hartland.'
parents.mother_research_notes: Anna Thompson Hooker not yet in tree.

career.location: West Hampton, Norwich, and Westfield Massachusetts
career.notes: Merchant at West Hampton MA, then Norwich MA. In 1819 removed family to Westfield MA, where they remained.

narrative_blocks.header: Moved family from Norwich Massachusetts to Westfield, 1819
narrative_blocks.body: Merchant at West Hampton MA before relocating to Norwich MA where his two eldest children Lucy Ashmun and William Throop were born. In 1819 he moved the family to Westfield MA, where son Edward was born 1822 and where William and his wife Octavia would both be buried at Pine Hill Cemetery.

narrative_blocks.header: Daughter Lucy Ashmun died at Rushville Illinois, 1839
narrative_blocks.body: His eldest child Lucy Ashmun died age 26 at Rushville IL April 17, 1839 — only seventeen months after her Massachusetts marriage to Dr Joseph Washburn Clark and five weeks after the birth of their infant son who himself died in May. The Illinois frontier had taken his daughter, son-in-law's first family, and grandson all within a year.

narrative_blocks.header: Died at Rushville Illinois eighteen months after marriage
narrative_blocks.body: Married Dr Joseph Washburn Clark of Rushville IL on her twenty-fifth birthday September 28, 1837 and moved with him to the Illinois frontier. Eighteen months later — five weeks after the April 12 birth of their only child William Hooker Clark — she died at Rushville on April 17, 1839. The infant followed her on May 6, 1839.

death.notes: Exact death date not in Sam-supplied sources.

career.organization: Farmers and Mechanics Bank of Hartford CT; then Continental Bank of New York City
career.location: Hartford CT, then New York City
career.notes: Cashier of the Farmers and Mechanics Bank of Hartford CT; later cashier of the Continental Bank of New York City.

narrative_blocks.header: Bank cashier in Hartford and New York City
narrative_blocks.body: Cashier of the Farmers and Mechanics Bank of Hartford CT, then cashier of the Continental Bank of New York City. Three of his six children — Henry Collins, William Augustus, and Caroline Christine — were born at Hartford while he held the Farmers and Mechanics post.

birth.notes: FindAGrave gives Westfield (family moved 1819 per EH H01410); EH 1909 has 'West hampton' likely a typo or family-origin reference. Using Westfield as more consistent with timeline.

death.cause: Mitral regurgitation of the heart (per death record)

career.organization: Phoenix Bank Hartford CT; Great Barrington bank; Westfield Bank
career.notes: Started as clerk in Phoenix Bank Hartford CT 1836 age 15; remained connected with that bank until 1847. Cashier of bank at Great Barrington MA 1847. Cashier of Westfield Bank, Westfield MA from 1851.

cross_connections.display_label: Mother of his wife Sarah Hall; his aunt through the Hale family
His uncle through the Hale family; married two Hooker women

narrative_blocks.header: Married his first cousin Sarah Hall, 1844
narrative_blocks.body: Married Sarah Hall (1819-1898) of Greenfield MA on October 10, 1844. Sarah's mother Sibylla Hale Hall was his own mother Octavia's sister — both daughters of Rev Enoch Hale of Westhampton — making the bride and groom first cousins and the marriage a second Hale-Hooker knot in the same generation.

narrative_blocks.header: Bank clerk at fifteen, Westfield Bank cashier
narrative_blocks.body: Became a clerk in the Phoenix Bank at Hartford CT in 1836 at age fifteen — staying eleven years before moving to Great Barrington MA as bank cashier in 1847. From 1851 he was cashier of the Westfield Bank in Westfield MA, where he remained until his death June 13, 1896 age 75.

death.notes: NOT TO BE CONFUSED with the unrelated 19th-c Commander Edward Hooker, US Navy.

career.notes: Banker in New York City until 1859.

narrative_blocks.header: New York City banker until 1859
narrative_blocks.body: Born Westfield MA the year after the family's 1819 move from Norwich MA, youngest of the four William Hooker Jr / Octavia Hale Hooker children. Married Georgeanna C. Loring December 22, 1852 and was a banker in New York City until 1859 — joining his elder brother William Throop in the city's banking world before leaving Wall Street near the panic year.

burial.plot_notes: Buried at his wife Georgiana's family cemetery — she was born Cincinnati OH 1827.

marriages.notes: Eleven children documented per FamilySearch: Sarah Hall (I01127, b.1819 m. Henry Hooker), John Richardson Hall (1821-1890), Charles Hall (1823-1849), Almira Hale Hall (1825-1889), William Hooker Hall (1827-1891), Mary Hall (1828), Edward Hall (1829), Isabella Hall (1832-1833 infant), Isabella Hall 2nd (1834-1853), Mary Eloise Hall (1837-1890), Capt Henry Throop Hall (1839-1897). Only Sarah Hall (I01127) built; other 10 deferred.

cross_connections.display_label: Her nephew who married her daughter Sarah Hall, 1844

narrative_blocks.header: Sister Octavia married into the Hooker line, 1811
narrative_blocks.body: Elder sister Octavia Hale married William Hooker Jr at Westhampton December 19, 1811 — eight years before Sybella's own 1818 marriage to Richardson Hall. The two sisters' lines knotted a second time when Sybella's eldest daughter Sarah married Octavia's own son Henry Hooker in October 1844, a first-cousin marriage between the two Hale-line branches.

narrative_blocks.header: Eleven children with Greenfield Massachusetts merchant
narrative_blocks.body: Married Richardson Hall of Norwich MA at Franklin County MA in 1818 and bore eleven children including Sarah Hall (1819-1900), Almira Hale Hall (1825-1889), William Hooker Hall (1827-1891), and Capt Henry Throop Hall (1839-1897). Two infants both named Isabella died in 1833 and 1853 respectively. She survived her husband by 24 years.

narrative_blocks.header: Sister wed William Hooker; daughter wed William's son
narrative_blocks.body: In October 1844, Sybella's daughter Sarah Hall married Henry Hooker — the son of Sybella's elder sister Octavia, who had herself married into the Hooker family thirty-three years earlier in 1811. Two generations of Hale women thus married into the same Westhampton Hooker household, completing a family bond begun by Nathan Hale's nieces.

death.notes: FindAGrave records May 23, 1900 Wellesley MA age 80. EH 1909 had given Oct 12, 1898 Cambridgeport MA — discrepancy; using FindAGrave as more reliable for vital records.

parents.father_research_notes: Rev. Enoch Hale (X02859), first minister of West Hampton MA, elder brother of Nathan Hale per EH 1909 #1410.
parents.mother_research_notes: Octavia Throop Hale — daughter of Rev. Enoch and Octavia Throop Hale of Bozrah CT per EH 1909 #1410.

career.notes: Per EH 1909 #1412: 'Enoch Hale, M.D.' Son of Rev. Enoch Hale, first minister of West Hampton.

cross_connections.display_label: Uncle; Continental Army spy executed by British, 1776
His nephew Henry Hooker married a Hale first cousin, 1844

narrative_blocks.header: Married two Hooker first cousins in sequence
narrative_blocks.body: After his first wife Almira Hooker died at Gardiner Maine in December 1816, Dr. Enoch Hale married Sarah Hooker in 1822 — Almira's own first cousin, daughter of Judge John Hooker of Springfield. Sarah died three years later in 1825; Enoch's third wife Jane Murdock, whom he married in 1827, survived him by forty-three years.

narrative_blocks.header: Nathan Hale's execution shadowed his Westhampton childhood
narrative_blocks.body: His father Rev. Enoch Hale was the elder brother of Capt. Nathan Hale, the Continental Army officer executed as a spy by the British in September 1776. Dr. Enoch Hale grew up in a household that lived under the shadow of his famous uncle's martyrdom.

bio.bio_blurb: Hooker daughter who married Dr. Enoch Hale

burial.plot_notes: Gravestone inscription: 'Almira, only Daughter of Dctr. William and Mrs. Anna Hooker, was born at Westhampton Nov. 10th 1792. She was married to Doctr. Enoch Hale Sept. 6th 1813 and died at Gardiner Dec. 5th 1816.'

cross_connections.display_label: First cousin Sarah Hooker became Enoch's second wife, 1822

narrative_blocks.header: Dead at twenty-three; niece bore her name
narrative_blocks.body: In September 1813, Almira married Enoch Hale, a physician and son of West Hampton's first minister — the same year her brother William had married Enoch's sister Octavia, binding two Hooker children and two Hale siblings in the same Westhampton community. Almira died at Gardiner Maine on December 5, 1816 at age twenty-four, only three years into the marriage.

narrative_blocks.header: Two Hookers married two Hales
narrative_blocks.body: In neighboring years a Hooker brother and sister each married a Hale: her brother William wed Octavia Hale in 1811, and she married Octavia’s brother, Dr. Enoch Hale, in 1813 — a double knot between two Westhampton families.

cross_connections.display_label: First cousin Almira was Dr. Enoch Hale's first wife

narrative_blocks.header: Married her first cousin Almira's widower, 1822
narrative_blocks.body: In May 1822, Sarah Hooker married Dr. Enoch Hale — the Westhampton physician who had been widowed six years earlier by her first cousin Almira Hooker. The two women shared the same Thomas Hooker lineage: Sarah through her father Judge John Hooker of Springfield, Almira through her father Dr. William Hooker of Westhampton. Sarah died at Westhampton three years later on April 25, 1825.

burialCemetery.name: Center Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Westhampton MA. Primary burial ground for the William Hooker / Henry Hooker / Kingsley / Drury / Bartlett branch. FindAGrave Cemetery #2198549.
